text:
BOTTOM LINE — Tech de-risking, defensives absorbed flow.
The tape repriced AI/semis lower after Broadcom’s weak chip outlook, and the 4.18% Nasdaq hit plus 5.64% SOXX drop showed forced selling in the fastest-momentum complex. Breadth rotated into Staples and Healthcare while small caps held up better than large caps, so this looked like a crowded-growth unwind rather than a full-risk liquidation.

INDICES — VIX 18.4, flat-to-up versus its 20-day 18.0.
Best to worst: IJR +0.78%, IWM +0.31%, DIA -0.29%, SPY -0.72%, QQQ -1.38%, SOXX -5.64%. STYLE: Small > large by 1.50pp; the sharpest cross-asset move was SLV +1.76%, followed by USO -3.50%.

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio and QQQ vol_ratio were not provided, so read-through is incomplete.
Attention clustered in IWM 1.8x, RSP 1.7x, IJR 1.3x, while DIA 0.8x, GLD 0.7x, and SLV 0.7x faded. Price-up with elevated volume in small caps signals accumulation, while price-up on lower volume in gold/silver signals a weaker bid.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLV Healthcare +3.03%, XLRE Real Estate +1.46%, XLP Staples +0.92%, XLY Discretionary +0.90%, XLU Utilities +0.76%, XLC Comms +0.57%, XLF Financials +0.22%, XLB Materials -0.46%, XLE Energy -0.46%, XLI Industrials -1.59%, XLK Technology -1.87%.
Top industries were WCLD +5.97% and IGV +4.06%; weakest were ICLN -3.89% and SOXX -5.64%. Money moved into Healthcare and Staples, while semis and industrials were sold; that is defensive risk-off.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol 2.30 and QQQ PCR_vol 1.07 both showed protection demand.
SPY skew +8.1% and QQQ skew +12.9% kept put premium elevated, while VIX 18.4 sat just above its 20-day 18.0, so hedging stayed active but not panicked. IWM PCR_vol 9.02 flagged extreme downside protection in small caps.

CROSS-ASSET — USO -3.50% and UUP -0.07% reinforced the growth scare.
Gold and silver both rose, with GLD +1.13% and SLV +1.76%, while equities sold off, which is a classic de-risking mix rather than inflation pricing.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed 62.3/100, with top movers SOXX -5.64% Semiconductors, XLK -1.87% Technology, and XLV +3.03% Healthcare; 20d momentum extremes were SOXX best and XLC worst among sectors, with IWM best and SPY worst among baskets.
ABBV 8.4x volume +4.20% in Healthcare was accumulation, while PWR 5.1x volume -4.28% in Buildout was distribution. Watch AMD -17%, Intel -9%, and Micron -12.6% for follow-through after the Broadcom-driven AI-chip reset; watch ABBV +4.20% for confirmation of defensive rotation; watch IWM +0.31% because 9.02 PCR_vol shows crowded downside hedging despite positive price.
